jokes.text: What's her name? What time do you want me there? 8:00.
jokes.setup: Bobby insists his Saturday date is as important as Elaine's, defending the relationship
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves when asked the girl's name, proving he doesn't even know it
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three lines that completely demolish Bobby's pretense. The quick capitulation is both surprising and character-revealing.

jokes.text: I could have got you to come in the cage if I'd have said her name, yeah.
jokes.setup: Louie admits he has relationship problems with Zena
jokes.punchline: Reveals he manipulated Alex by not mentioning it was about romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - scheming and self-aware about his manipulation tactics

jokes.text: You're not pouring your guts out to me. You're telling me that you're not getting enough action. Insatiable lust doesn't make you sympathetic.
jokes.setup: Louie claims to be pouring his guts out about relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Alex cuts through the euphemisms to the crude reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that exposes Louie's self-serving framing while maintaining Alex's moral clarity

jokes.text: Zena sees something in me that no other woman ever saw... That no other person ever saw... That isn't there.
jokes.setup: Louie gets sentimental about Zena seeing something special in him
jokes.punchline: The devastating self-aware twist that it's imaginary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with perfect character insight. The escalation from 'woman' to 'person' builds expectation before the devastating reversal.

jokes.text: You mean, like, uh, if the house looks clean I could say, 'Hey, the place looks great.' Sure. That was my idea. I thought of the words. You just said, 'talk.' I got the golden key here.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Louie compliment Zena's housekeeping as conversation
jokes.punchline: Louie takes credit for the specific words and thinks he's discovered the secret to romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie behavior - taking credit for obvious things and overestimating his insights

jokes.text: Gee, the place looks immaculate.
jokes.setup: Louie learned to compliment Zena's housekeeping
jokes.punchline: Uses an over-the-top word choice that sounds unnatural from him
jokes.explanation: 'Immaculate' is hilariously wrong for Louie's vocabulary and shows his clumsy attempt at sophistication

jokes.text: Trying to romance you is like trying to blow your nose without a handkerchief.
jokes.setup: Zena expresses frustration with Louie's romantic efforts
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and disgusting analogy
jokes.explanation: Vivid, disgusting imagery that perfectly captures the awkwardness and mess of Louie's romantic attempts

jokes.text: 87 bucks for a French meal you don't want to eat, they don't want me to take home. I got to go out and find my own bags at 8:00 at night.
jokes.setup: Louie complains about his expensive romantic evening
jokes.punchline: The detail about having to find his own doggy bags shows his cheapness
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - even when trying to be romantic, Louie can't help being petty and cheap

jokes.text: Boy, this place looks immaculate.
jokes.setup: Louie tries the housekeeping compliment again after being rejected
jokes.punchline: The tone-deaf repetition makes it even more awkward
jokes.explanation: The desperate repetition of his one 'romantic' line shows how clueless he is about reading the room

jokes.text: I know what'll take the tension out of you. Oh, no. No.
jokes.setup: Zena says she's tense and needs to be alone
jokes.punchline: Louie suggests sex and Zena's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment showing Louie's tone-deafness, though relies on obvious sexual humor

jokes.text: Please let me be deaf.
jokes.setup: Louie asks if she still loves him
jokes.punchline: Zena's prayer to not have to hear his response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - captures Zena's dread at having to break up with him in just five words

jokes.text: I'm sure you don't want to know the details. No, you don't, really.
jokes.setup: Zena mentions her relationship with another man developed naturally
jokes.punchline: She offers not to give details, then confirms he really doesn't want them
jokes.explanation: The awkward kindness of trying to spare him details while confirming his worst fears

jokes.text: I can't... breathe!
jokes.setup: Zena finishes explaining how she fell for another man
jokes.punchline: Louie has a dramatic physical reaction to the news
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reaction but fits Louie's dramatic personality

jokes.text: Someday... you're going to come back to me... Crawling on your knees, begging me to take you back... And when you do... you know what I'll say? I'll say... thank God.
jokes.setup: Louie builds up to what sounds like a vengeful rejection fantasy
jokes.punchline: Instead reveals he'd immediately take her back with gratitude
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection that subverts the expected tough-guy posturing with touching vulnerability. Classic Taxi writing.

jokes.text: You said 8:00, right?
jokes.setup: Bobby earlier agreed to babysit but now wants to back out for his important date
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for the time after mentioning Gloria's name, showing he's still committed despite his protests
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Bobby's inability to actually follow through on his tough stance

jokes.text: There will be soon. I put a quarter in the jukebox. No. You just put a quarter in the cigarette machine.
jokes.setup: Jim asks Elaine to dance, saying there will be music soon from the jukebox
jokes.punchline: He actually put money in the cigarette machine instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Jim's absent-mindedness in a specific, believable way

jokes.text: Well, Elaine, would you care to smoke?
jokes.setup: Jim realizes he put money in the wrong machine
jokes.punchline: He smoothly pivots to asking if she wants to smoke instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan recovery that shows Jim's character - he doesn't get flustered, just adapts to absurd situations

jokes.text: I'm meeting you guys here for breakfast. They haven't played my cigarette yet.
jokes.setup: Tony says he needs to leave early to meet them for breakfast the next morning
jokes.punchline: Jim refers to his cigarettes as music that hasn't 'played' yet
jokes.explanation: Jim's continued confusion about the machines creates a nice absurdist moment

jokes.text: The worst part about it is I kept Ma up all night too. Why was that? What do you want me to do? Talk to the walls?
jokes.setup: Louie says he kept his mother awake while drinking all night after Zena left
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was talking to his mother because he had no one else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - his loneliness masked as practical logic about needing someone to talk to

jokes.text: What am I supposed to do, here? My life is falling apart so I should listen to stories about you? I mean, you know, let's put the little selfishness in check here.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to relate to Louie's heartbreak with his own similar experience
jokes.punchline: Louie accuses Alex of being selfish for trying to share his own story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Louie displays massive selfishness while lecturing about putting selfishness in check

jokes.text: Look out, Dwight! I think it's him.
jokes.setup: Louie wants to identify Dwight, the bartender who stole Zena
jokes.punchline: He yells at the bartender and the man's reaction confirms his identity
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that works for the situation, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: Pump the scum! No pumping. Pump him! No pumping. Pump the scum! Just beer! Pump the scum! No! Just beer! Pump him! Pump the scum! Sit! Stay! Stay. Stay.
jokes.setup: Louie wants Alex to interrogate Dwight while getting beer
jokes.punchline: The argument escalates into Alex treating Louie like a dog with commands
jokes.explanation: Good escalating physical comedy though relies heavily on repetition for impact

jokes.text: I'm torn between joy... and rapture.
jokes.setup: Louie learns that Dwight is going to break up with Zena
jokes.punchline: Instead of mixed feelings, he lists two positive emotions as his internal conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of the 'torn between two feelings' trope - shows Louie's selfishness with clever wordplay

jokes.text: It's what separates us from the animals.
jokes.setup: Louie philosophizes about how everyone destroys everyone else
jokes.punchline: Claims this mutual destruction is what makes humans special
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant inversion of typical 'what separates us from animals' sentiments - perfectly cynical Louie

jokes.text: Maybe a teeny bit.
jokes.setup: Louie says he was going to make Zena beg but can't now
jokes.punchline: Admits he still wants her to beg just a little
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie can't completely let go of his petty desires even when being 'magnanimous'

jokes.text: What do you mean? / It's all you ever think about. / You mean, that's the problem?
jokes.setup: Zena lists Louie's sexual problem among his many issues
jokes.punchline: Louie's genuine confusion about why thinking about sex constantly would be a problem
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's complete lack of self-awareness about what others consider problems. His confusion is genuine and funny.

jokes.text: Love... is the end of happiness. Because one day, all a guy's got to do to be happy is to watch the Mets. The next day, you got to have Zena in the room watching the Mets with you.
jokes.setup: Zena asks Louie to define love
jokes.punchline: His cynical but oddly touching definition based on needing someone there for simple pleasures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific wisdom that's both cynical and romantic. The Mets detail makes it authentically Louie while being universally relatable.

jokes.text: Well, I can't watch them now, anyway, because it's the middle of the winter. But the philosophy's the same.
jokes.setup: Louie's romantic speech about watching the Mets together
jokes.punchline: Zena's practical observation about baseball season being over
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflating of Louie's romantic moment with practical reality, followed by her acceptance of his 'philosophy'

jokes.text: Here I am, back in the same old stinking, lousy, crummy, rotten relationship all over again... The same place I was in to begin with. I'll never get out from under it. Oh, Zena, you don't know how happy that makes me feel.
jokes.setup: Louie complains bitterly about being back in his old relationship
jokes.punchline: Reveals this terrible situation makes him incredibly happy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that captures Louie's contradictory nature - he knows the relationship is awful but can't help being thrilled to have it back
